title: OnFly: Onboard Zero-Shot Aerial Vision-Language Navigation toward Safety and Efficiency
summary: Aerial vision-language navigation (AVLN) enables UAVs to follow natural-language instructions in complex 3D environments. However, existing zero-shot AVLN methods often suffer from unstable single-stream Vision-Language Model decision-making, unreliable long-horizon progress monitoring, and a trade-off between safety and efficiency. We propose OnFly, a fully onboard, real-time framework for zero-shot AVLN. OnFly adopts a shared-perception dual-agent architecture that decouples high-frequency target generation from low-frequency progress monitoring, thereby stabilizing decision-making. It further employs a hybrid keyframe-recent-frame memory to preserve global trajectory context while maintaining KV-cache prefix stability, enabling reliable long-horizon monitoring with termination and recovery signals. In addition, a semantic-geometric verifier refines VLM-predicted targets for instruction consistency and geometric safety using VLM features and depth cues, while a receding-horizon planner generates optimized collision-free trajectories under geometric safety constraints, improving both safety and efficiency. In simulation, OnFly improves task success from 26.4% to 67.8%, compared with the strongest state-of-the-art baseline, while fully onboard real-world flights validate its feasibility for real-time deployment. The code will be released at https://github.com/Robotics-STAR-Lab/OnFly
tldr: The paper introduces OnFly, a real-time, fully onboard framework for zero-shot aerial vision-language navigation that significantly improves task success and safety using a dual-agent architecture, hybrid memory, and semantic-geometric verification.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为OnFly的实时、完全板载的零样本空中视觉语言导航框架，该框架采用双代理架构、混合记忆和语义几何验证，显著提高了任务成功率和安全性。
summary_zh: 无人机视觉语言导航 (AVLN) 使无人机能够在复杂的 3D 环境中遵循自然语言指令。然而，现有的零样本 AVLN 方法通常面临着单流视觉语言模型决策不稳定、长时程进度监控不可靠以及安全性和效率之间的权衡等问题。我们提出了 OnFly，一个完全板载的、实时的零样本 AVLN 框架。OnFly 采用共享感知的双智能体架构，将高频目标生成与低频进度监控解耦，从而稳定决策过程。它进一步利用混合关键帧-最近帧记忆来保存全局轨迹上下文，同时保持 KV-cache 前缀的稳定性，从而实现可靠的长时程监控并具备终止和恢复信号。此外，语义-几何验证器利用视觉语言模型特征和深度提示，对视觉语言模型预测的目标进行精细化处理，以保证指令一致性和几何安全性，而递推时域规划器则在几何安全约束下生成优化的无碰撞轨迹，从而提高安全性和效率。在模拟中，与最先进的基线相比，OnFly 将任务成功率从 26.4% 提高到 67.8%，同时完全板载的真实世界飞行验证了其实时部署的可行性。代码将在 https://github.com/Robotics-STAR-Lab/OnFly 发布。

title: ResWM: Residual-Action World Model for Visual RL
summary: Learning predictive world models from raw visual observations is a central challenge in reinforcement learning (RL), especially for robotics and continuous control. Conventional model-based RL frameworks directly condition future predictions on absolute actions, which makes optimization unstable: the optimal action distributions are task-dependent, unknown a priori, and often lead to oscillatory or inefficient control. To address this, we introduce the Residual-Action World Model (ResWM), a new framework that reformulates the control variable from absolute actions to residual actions -- incremental adjustments relative to the previous step. This design aligns with the inherent smoothness of real-world control, reduces the effective search space, and stabilizes long-horizon planning. To further strengthen the representation, we propose an Observation Difference Encoder that explicitly models the changes between adjacent frames, yielding compact latent dynamics that are naturally coupled with residual actions. ResWM is integrated into a Dreamer-style latent dynamics model with minimal modifications and no extra hyperparameters. Both imagination rollouts and policy optimization are conducted in the residual-action space, enabling smoother exploration, lower control variance, and more reliable planning. Empirical results on the DeepMind Control Suite demonstrate that ResWM achieves consistent improvements in sample efficiency, asymptotic returns, and control smoothness, significantly surpassing strong baselines such as Dreamer and TD-MPC. Beyond performance, ResWM produces more stable and energy-efficient action trajectories, a property critical for robotic systems deployed in real-world environments. These findings suggest that residual action modeling provides a simple yet powerful principle for bridging algorithmic advances in RL with the practical requirements of robotics.
tldr: This paper introduces ResWM, a residual-action world model for visual RL that improves sample efficiency and control smoothness by using incremental action adjustments and an observation difference encoder, achieving significant improvements over Dreamer and TD-MPC on the DeepMind Control Suite.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了ResWM，一种用于视觉强化学习的残差动作世界模型，通过使用增量动作调整和观察差异编码器来提高样本效率和控制平滑度，并在DeepMind控制套件上实现了优于Dreamer和TD-MPC的显著改进。
summary_zh: 从原始视觉观测中学习预测性的世界模型是强化学习（RL）的核心挑战，特别是对于机器人学和连续控制而言。传统的基于模型的强化学习框架直接以绝对动作为条件进行未来预测，这导致优化不稳定：最优动作分布是任务相关的、先验未知的，并且通常会导致振荡或低效的控制。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了残差动作世界模型（ResWM），这是一种新的框架，它将控制变量从绝对动作重新定义为残差动作——相对于前一步的增量调整。这种设计与现实世界控制的固有平滑性相符，减小了有效的搜索空间，并稳定了长程规划。为了进一步加强表征，我们提出了一种观测差异编码器，它显式地建模相邻帧之间的变化，从而产生与残差动作自然耦合的紧凑潜在动力学。ResWM以最小的修改和零额外超参数集成到类Dreamer的潜在动力学模型中。想象力展开和策略优化都在残差动作空间中进行，从而实现更平滑的探索、更低的控制方差和更可靠的规划。在DeepMind控制套件上的实验结果表明，ResWM在样本效率、渐近回报和控制平滑性方面都取得了持续的改进，显著超越了Dreamer和TD-MPC等强大的基线。除了性能之外，ResWM还生成了更稳定和节能的动作轨迹，这一特性对于部署在真实世界环境中的机器人系统至关重要。这些发现表明，残差动作建模为弥合强化学习算法进步与机器人学实际需求之间的差距提供了一个简单而强大的原则。

title: A Survey of Reasoning in Autonomous Driving Systems: Open Challenges and Emerging Paradigms
summary: The development of high-level autonomous driving (AD) is shifting from perception-centric limitations to a more fundamental bottleneck, namely, a deficit in robust and generalizable reasoning. Although current AD systems manage structured environments, they consistently falter in long-tail scenarios and complex social interactions that require human-like judgment. Meanwhile, the advent of large language and multimodal models (LLMs and MLLMs) presents a transformative opportunity to integrate a powerful cognitive engine into AD systems, moving beyond pattern matching toward genuine comprehension. However, a systematic framework to guide this integration is critically lacking. To bridge this gap, we provide a comprehensive review of this emerging field and argue that reasoning should be elevated from a modular component to the system's cognitive core. Specifically, we first propose a novel Cognitive Hierarchy to decompose the monolithic driving task according to its cognitive and interactive complexity. Building on this, we further derive and systematize seven core reasoning challenges, such as the responsiveness-reasoning trade-off and social-game reasoning. Furthermore, we conduct a dual-perspective review of the state-of-the-art, analyzing both system-centric approaches to architecting intelligent agents and evaluation-centric practices for their validation. Our analysis reveals a clear trend toward holistic and interpretable "glass-box" agents. In conclusion, we identify a fundamental and unresolved tension between the high-latency, deliberative nature of LLM-based reasoning and the millisecond-scale, safety-critical demands of vehicle control. For future work, a primary objective is to bridge the symbolic-to-physical gap by developing verifiable neuro-symbolic architectures, robust reasoning under uncertainty, and scalable models for implicit social negotiation.
tldr: This paper surveys the emerging field of reasoning in autonomous driving, arguing for a shift toward reasoning as the cognitive core of AD systems and exploring the integration of LLMs/MLLMs. It identifies challenges and future directions, including verifiable neuro-symbolic architectures.
tldr_zh: 本文调研了自动驾驶中推理的新兴领域，认为应该将推理转变为自动驾驶系统的认知核心，并探讨了LLM/MLLM的集成。文章确定了挑战和未来方向，包括可验证的神经符号架构。
summary_zh: 高级自动驾驶（AD）的发展正从以感知为中心的局限性转向一个更根本的瓶颈，即稳健且可泛化的推理能力不足。尽管当前的AD系统能够处理结构化的环境，但它们在需要类人判断的长尾场景和复杂的社会互动中却屡屡出错。与此同时，大型语言模型和多模态模型（LLMs和MLLMs）的出现为将强大的认知引擎集成到AD系统中提供了一个变革性的机会，从而超越了模式匹配，迈向真正的理解。然而，目前严重缺乏一个指导这种集成的系统性框架。为了弥补这一差距，我们对这一新兴领域进行了全面的综述，并认为推理应该从一个模块化的组件提升到系统的认知核心。具体来说，我们首先提出了一个新颖的认知层次结构，以根据其认知和交互复杂性来分解一体化的驾驶任务。在此基础上，我们进一步推导并系统化了七个核心推理挑战，例如响应性-推理权衡和社会博弈推理。此外，我们对最先进的技术进行了双重视角的评述，分析了以系统为中心构建智能体的方法以及以评估为中心的智能体验证实践。我们的分析揭示了一个清晰的趋势，即朝着整体性和可解释性的“玻璃盒”智能体发展。总而言之，我们发现基于LLM的推理的高延迟、深思熟虑的特性与车辆控制的毫秒级、安全关键需求之间存在着一个根本且未解决的矛盾。对于未来的工作，主要目标是通过开发可验证的神经符号架构、不确定性下的稳健推理以及用于隐式社会协商的可扩展模型来弥合符号到物理的鸿沟。

title: DepthCache: Depth-Guided Training-Free Visual Token Merging for Vision-Language-Action Model Inference
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models enable generalist robotic manipulation but suffer from high inference latency. This bottleneck stems from the massive number of visual tokens processed by large language backbones. Existing methods either prune or merge tokens uniformly, degrading the spatial reasoning essential for robotic control. We present DepthCache, a training-free framework that leverages depth as a structural prior for visual token compression. It partitions observations into depth-based regions and applies spatially differentiated merge ratios, preserving the near-field workspace while compressing the distant background. To exploit temporal redundancy, DepthCache distributes the merging process across consecutive frames, ensuring consistent representations while reducing per-step computation. A motion-adaptive pipeline further optimizes auxiliary view compression based on end-effector dynamics. The framework requires no model modification, generalizing across diverse VLA architectures. On the LIBERO benchmark, DepthCache achieves up to 1.28x inference speedup with less than 1% average success rate degradation across three VLA models (pi_0.5, OpenVLA, GR00T), whereas pruning and merging baselines incur 4--24% degradation at comparable compression. Real-world experiments on a physical manipulator demonstrate that DepthCache enables faster task throughput and more responsive closed-loop control in latency-sensitive scenarios.
tldr: DepthCache is a training-free visual token merging framework that utilizes depth information to accelerate inference in VLA models for robotics by selectively compressing visual tokens based on depth and motion, improving speed without significant performance degradation.
tldr_zh: DepthCache是一个免训练的视觉令牌合并框架，它利用深度信息来加速VLA模型在机器人技术中的推理。该框架通过基于深度和运动选择性地压缩视觉令牌，从而在不显著降低性能的情况下提高速度。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型实现了通用机器人操作，但存在推理延迟高的问题。这个瓶颈源于大型语言骨干网络处理的大量视觉标记。现有方法要么均匀地剪枝或合并标记，从而降低了机器人控制所必需的空间推理能力。我们提出了DepthCache，这是一个无需训练的框架，它利用深度作为视觉标记压缩的结构化先验。它将观测结果划分为基于深度的区域，并应用空间上不同的合并比例，保留近场工作空间，同时压缩远处的背景。为了利用时间冗余，DepthCache将合并过程分布在连续帧上，确保一致的表示，同时减少每步计算量。一个运动自适应流水线进一步基于末端执行器动力学优化辅助视图压缩。该框架无需模型修改，可推广到各种VLA架构。在LIBERO基准测试中，DepthCache实现了高达1.28倍的推理加速，并且在三个VLA模型（pi_0.5、OpenVLA、GR00T）上的平均成功率降幅小于1%，而剪枝和合并基线在相当的压缩率下会产生4-24%的降幅。在物理机械臂上的真实世界实验表明，DepthCache能够在延迟敏感场景中实现更快的任务吞吐量和更具响应性的闭环控制。

title: DiT4DiT: Jointly Modeling Video Dynamics and Actions for Generalizable Robot Control
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have emerged as a promising paradigm for robot learning, but their representations are still largely inherited from static image-text pretraining, leaving physical dynamics to be learned from comparatively limited action data. Generative video models, by contrast, encode rich spatiotemporal structure and implicit physics, making them a compelling foundation for robotic manipulation. But their potentials are not fully explored in the literature. To bridge the gap, we introduce DiT4DiT, an end-to-end Video-Action Model that couples a video Diffusion Transformer with an action Diffusion Transformer in a unified cascaded framework. Instead of relying on reconstructed future frames, DiT4DiT extracts intermediate denoising features from the video generation process and uses them as temporally grounded conditions for action prediction. We further propose a dual flow-matching objective with decoupled timesteps and noise scales for video prediction, hidden-state extraction, and action inference, enabling coherent joint training of both modules. Across simulation and real-world benchmarks, DiT4DiT achieves state-of-the-art results, reaching average success rates of 98.6% on LIBERO and 50.8% on RoboCasa GR1 while using substantially less training data. On the Unitree G1 robot, it also delivers superior real-world performance and strong zero-shot generalization. Importantly, DiT4DiT improves sample efficiency by over 10x and speeds up convergence by up to 7x, demonstrating that video generation can serve as an effective scaling proxy for robot policy learning. We release code and models at https://dit4dit.github.io/.
tldr: The paper introduces DiT4DiT, a novel Video-Action model leveraging video diffusion transformers for robot control. It couples a video diffusion transformer with an action diffusion transformer, achieving substantial improvements in sample efficiency, convergence speed, and generalization.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了DiT4DiT，一种新颖的视频-动作模型，利用视频扩散变换器进行机器人控制。它将视频扩散变换器与动作扩散变换器相结合，在样本效率、收敛速度和泛化能力方面取得了显著提升。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型已成为机器人学习领域一种很有前景的范式，但其表征在很大程度上仍然继承自静态图像-文本预训练，使得物理动力学只能从相对有限的动作数据中学习。相反，生成式视频模型编码了丰富的时空结构和隐式物理，使其成为机器人操作的强大基础。但是，它们在文献中的潜力尚未得到充分探索。为了弥合这一差距，我们引入了 DiT4DiT，一个端到端的视频-动作模型，它在一个统一的级联框架中将视频扩散 Transformer 与动作扩散 Transformer 耦合在一起。DiT4DiT 不是依赖于重建的未来帧，而是从视频生成过程中提取中间去噪特征，并将其用作时间上接地的动作预测条件。我们进一步提出了一种具有解耦时间步长和噪声尺度的双流匹配目标，用于视频预测、隐藏状态提取和动作推断，从而实现两个模块的连贯联合训练。在模拟和真实世界基准测试中，DiT4DiT 取得了最先进的结果，在 LIBERO 上达到了 98.6% 的平均成功率，在 RoboCasa GR1 上达到了 50.8% 的平均成功率，同时使用了明显更少的训练数据。在 Unitree G1 机器人上，它还提供了卓越的真实世界性能和强大的零样本泛化能力。重要的是，DiT4DiT 将样本效率提高了 10 倍以上，并将收敛速度提高了 7 倍，这表明视频生成可以作为机器人策略学习的有效扩展代理。我们在 https://dit4dit.github.io/ 上发布了代码和模型。

title: KnowDiffuser: A Knowledge-Guided Diffusion Planner with LM Reasoning and Prior-Informed Trajectory Initialization
summary: Recent advancements in Language Models (LMs) have demonstrated strong semantic reasoning capabilities, enabling their application in high-level decision-making for autonomous driving (AD). However, LMs operate over discrete token spaces and lack the ability to generate continuous, physically feasible trajectories required for motion planning. Meanwhile, diffusion models have proven effective at generating reliable and dynamically consistent trajectories, but often lack semantic interpretability and alignment with scene-level understanding. To address these limitations, we propose \textbf{KnowDiffuser}, a knowledge-guided motion planning framework that tightly integrates the semantic understanding of language models with the generative power of diffusion models. The framework employs a language model to infer context-aware meta-actions from structured scene representations, which are then mapped to prior trajectories that anchor the subsequent denoising process. A two-stage truncated denoising mechanism refines these trajectories efficiently, preserving both semantic alignment and physical feasibility. Experiments on the nuPlan benchmark demonstrate that KnowDiffuser significantly outperforms existing planners in both open-loop and closed-loop evaluations, establishing a robust and interpretable framework that effectively bridges the semantic-to-physical gap in AD systems.
tldr: KnowDiffuser combines Language Models and Diffusion Models for autonomous driving, using LM reasoning for meta-actions and diffusion for trajectory generation, achieving superior performance on the nuPlan benchmark.
tldr_zh: KnowDiffuser结合了语言模型和扩散模型用于自动驾驶，利用LM推理进行元动作决策，并使用扩散模型生成轨迹，在nuPlan基准测试中表现优异。
summary_zh: 语言模型 (LM) 近期的进展已经展示了强大的语义推理能力，使其能够应用于自动驾驶 (AD) 的高层决策。然而，语言模型在离散的 token 空间中运行，缺乏生成运动规划所需的连续、物理可行的轨迹的能力。与此同时，扩散模型在生成可靠且动态一致的轨迹方面已被证明是有效的，但通常缺乏语义可解释性以及与场景级理解的对齐。为了解决这些局限性，我们提出了 \textbf{KnowDiffuser}，一个知识引导的运动规划框架，它将语言模型的语义理解与扩散模型的生成能力紧密结合。该框架采用语言模型从结构化的场景表示中推断出上下文感知的元动作，然后将其映射到先验轨迹，以此作为后续去噪过程的锚点。一个两阶段的截断去噪机制可以高效地优化这些轨迹，同时保留语义对齐和物理可行性。在 nuPlan 基准上的实验表明，KnowDiffuser 在开环和闭环评估中均显著优于现有的规划器，从而建立了一个鲁棒且可解释的框架，有效地弥合了自动驾驶系统中语义到物理的鸿沟。

title: Overcoming Visual Clutter in Vision Language Action Models via Concept-Gated Visual Distillation
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models demonstrate impressive zero-shot generalization but frequently suffer from a "Precision-Reasoning Gap" in cluttered environments. This failure is driven by background-induced feature dilution, where high-frequency semantic noise corrupts the geometric grounding required for precise manipulation. To bridge this gap, we propose Concept-Gated Visual Distillation (CGVD), a training-free, model-agnostic inference framework that stabilizes VLA policies. CGVD operates by parsing instructions into safe and distractor sets, utilizing a two-layer target refinement process--combining cross-validation and spatial disambiguation--to explicitly penalize false positives and isolate genuine manipulation targets. We then process the scene via Fourier-based inpainting, generating a clean observation that actively suppresses semantic distractors while preserving critical spatial geometry and visual proprioception. Extensive evaluations in highly cluttered manipulation tasks demonstrate that CGVD prevents performance collapse. In environments with dense semantic distractors, our method significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, achieving a 77.5% success rate compared to the baseline's 43.0%. By enforcing strict attribute adherence, CGVD establishes inference-time visual distillation as a critical prerequisite for robust robotic manipulation in the clutter.
tldr: The paper introduces Concept-Gated Visual Distillation (CGVD), a training-free, model-agnostic inference framework, to address the "Precision-Reasoning Gap" in vision-language-action models caused by visual clutter, achieving significant performance improvements in cluttered robotic manipulation tasks.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为概念门控视觉蒸馏（CGVD）的免训练、模型无关的推理框架，旨在解决视觉-语言-动作模型中由视觉杂乱引起的“精确推理差距”，并在杂乱的机器人操作任务中实现了显著的性能提升。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型展现出令人印象深刻的零样本泛化能力，但经常在杂乱环境中遭受“精度-推理差距”。这种失败是由背景诱导的特征稀释驱动的，其中高频语义噪声会破坏精确定位所需的几何基础。为了弥合这一差距，我们提出了一种概念门控视觉蒸馏（CGVD），这是一种无需训练、与模型无关的推理框架，可以稳定 VLA 策略。CGVD 的工作方式是将指令解析为安全集和干扰集，并利用一个两层目标精炼过程——结合交叉验证和空间消歧——来显式地惩罚假阳性并隔离真正的操作目标。然后，我们通过基于傅立叶的图像修复处理场景，生成一个干净的观测，主动抑制语义干扰因素，同时保留关键的空间几何结构和视觉本体感受。在高度杂乱的操作任务中进行的大量评估表明，CGVD 可以防止性能崩溃。在具有密集语义干扰物的环境中，我们的方法显著优于最先进的基线，成功率达到 77.5%，而基线的成功率为 43.0%。通过强制执行严格的属性遵守，CGVD 确定了推理时的视觉蒸馏是实现杂乱环境中鲁棒机器人操作的关键前提。

title: Hybrid Self-evolving Structured Memory for GUI Agents
summary: The remarkable progress of vision-language models (VLMs) has enabled GUI agents to interact with computers in a human-like manner. Yet real-world computer-use tasks remain difficult due to long-horizon workflows, diverse interfaces, and frequent intermediate errors. Prior work equips agents with external memory built from large collections of trajectories, but relies on flat retrieval over discrete summaries or continuous embeddings, falling short of the structured organization and self-evolving characteristics of human memory. Inspired by the brain, we propose Hybrid Self-evolving Structured Memory (HyMEM), a graph-based memory that couples discrete high-level symbolic nodes with continuous trajectory embeddings. HyMEM maintains a graph structure to support multi-hop retrieval, self-evolution via node update operations, and on-the-fly working-memory refreshing during inference. Extensive experiments show that HyMEM consistently improves open-source GUI agents, enabling 7B/8B backbones to match or surpass strong closed-source models; notably, it boosts Qwen2.5-VL-7B by +22.5% and outperforms Gemini2.5-Pro-Vision and GPT-4o.
tldr: The paper introduces HyMEM, a hybrid graph-based memory for GUI agents that combines discrete symbolic nodes with continuous trajectory embeddings, improving their performance on long-horizon, real-world computer tasks and surpassing strong closed-source models.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了HyMEM，一种用于GUI代理的混合图结构记忆，它结合了离散符号节点和连续轨迹嵌入，提升了代理在长程、真实世界计算机任务上的表现，并超越了强大的闭源模型。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言模型（VLMs）的显著进展使得GUI智能体能够以类似人类的方式与计算机交互。然而，由于长时程工作流、多样界面和频繁的中间错误，现实世界的计算机使用任务仍然具有挑战性。先前的工作为智能体配备了从大量轨迹构建的外部记忆，但依赖于离散摘要或连续嵌入的扁平检索，缺乏人类记忆的结构化组织和自演化特征。受大脑启发，我们提出了混合自演化结构化记忆（HyMEM），一种基于图的记忆，将离散的高层符号节点与连续的轨迹嵌入相结合。HyMEM维护一个图结构，以支持多跳检索、通过节点更新操作实现的自演化，以及推理期间的即时工作记忆刷新。大量实验表明，HyMEM持续改进开源GUI智能体，使得7B/8B骨干模型能够匹配或超越强大的闭源模型；值得注意的是，它使Qwen2.5-VL-7B提升了+22.5%，并优于Gemini2.5-Pro-Vision和GPT-4o。

title: DIVE: Scaling Diversity in Agentic Task Synthesis for Generalizable Tool Use
summary: Recent work synthesizes agentic tasks for post-training tool-using LLMs, yet robust generalization under shifts in tasks and toolsets remains an open challenge. We trace this brittleness to insufficient diversity in synthesized tasks. Scaling diversity is difficult because training requires tasks to remain executable and verifiable, while generalization demands coverage of diverse tool types, toolset combinations, and heterogeneous tool-use patterns. We propose DIVE, an evidence-driven recipe that inverts synthesis order, executing diverse, real-world tools first and reverse-deriving tasks strictly entailed by the resulting traces, thereby providing grounding by construction. DIVE scales structural diversity along two controllable axes, tool-pool coverage and per-task toolset variety, and an Evidence Collection--Task Derivation loop further induces rich multi-step tool-use patterns across 373 tools in five domains. Training Qwen3-8B on DIVE data (48k SFT + 3.2k RL) improves by +22 average points across 9 OOD benchmarks and outperforms the strongest 8B baseline by +68. Remarkably, controlled scaling analysis reveals that diversity scaling consistently outperforms quantity scaling for OOD generalization, even with 4x less data.
tldr: The paper introduces DIVE, a method for synthesizing diverse agentic tasks for training tool-using LLMs, improving out-of-distribution generalization by inverting the synthesis order to derive tasks from real-world tool execution traces. Results show that diversity scaling is more important than quantity scaling.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了DIVE，一种用于合成多样化的智能体任务的方法，用于训练使用工具的大型语言模型。该方法通过颠倒合成顺序，从真实世界工具执行轨迹中派生任务，从而提高了域外泛化能力。结果表明，多样性扩展比数量扩展更重要。
summary_zh: 近期工作致力于为后训练工具使用型大型语言模型合成自主任务，然而，在任务和工具集发生变化的情况下实现稳健泛化仍然是一个开放的挑战。我们将这种脆弱性归咎于合成任务的多样性不足。扩展多样性是困难的，因为训练要求任务保持可执行和可验证，而泛化则要求覆盖多样化的工具类型、工具集组合以及异构的工具使用模式。我们提出了一种证据驱动的配方DIVE，它颠倒了合成顺序，首先执行多样化的真实世界工具，然后反向推导出严格由此产生的轨迹所蕴含的任务，从而通过构造提供基础。DIVE沿着两个可控轴扩展结构多样性：工具池覆盖率和每个任务的工具集多样性，并且一个“证据收集——任务推导”循环进一步诱导了跨越五个领域的373个工具中丰富的多步工具使用模式。使用DIVE数据（48k SFT + 3.2k RL）训练Qwen3-8B在9个OOD基准测试中平均提高了+22个点，并且优于最强的8B基线+68个点。值得注意的是，受控的缩放分析显示，对于OOD泛化，多样性缩放始终优于数量缩放，即使数据量减少了4倍。

title: WalkGPT: Grounded Vision-Language Conversation with Depth-Aware Segmentation for Pedestrian Navigation
summary: Ensuring accessible pedestrian navigation requires reasoning about both semantic and spatial aspects of complex urban scenes, a challenge that existing Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) struggle to meet. Although these models can describe visual content, their lack of explicit grounding leads to object hallucinations and unreliable depth reasoning, limiting their usefulness for accessibility guidance. We introduce WalkGPT, a pixel-grounded LVLM for the new task of Grounded Navigation Guide, unifying language reasoning and segmentation within a single architecture for depth-aware accessibility guidance. Given a pedestrian-view image and a navigation query, WalkGPT generates a conversational response with segmentation masks that delineate accessible and harmful features, along with relative depth estimation. The model incorporates a Multi-Scale Query Projector (MSQP) that shapes the final image tokens by aggregating them along text tokens across spatial hierarchies, and a Calibrated Text Projector (CTP), guided by a proposed Region Alignment Loss, that maps language embeddings into segmentation-aware representations. These components enable fine-grained grounding and depth inference without user-provided cues or anchor points, allowing the model to generate complete and realistic navigation guidance. We also introduce PAVE, a large-scale benchmark of 41k pedestrian-view images paired with accessibility-aware questions and depth-grounded answers. Experiments show that WalkGPT achieves strong grounded reasoning and segmentation performance. The source code and dataset are available on the \href{https://sites.google.com/view/walkgpt-26/home}{project website}.
tldr: The paper introduces WalkGPT, a pixel-grounded LVLM with depth-aware segmentation for pedestrian navigation, addressing limitations of existing LVLMs in spatial reasoning and introducing a new benchmark dataset, PAVE.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了WalkGPT，一个具有深度感知分割的像素级视觉-语言模型，用于行人导航，解决了现有视觉-语言模型在空间推理方面的局限性，并引入了一个新的基准数据集PAVE。
summary_zh: 确保无障碍行人导航需要在复杂的城市场景中进行语义和空间方面的推理，这对现有的大型视觉语言模型(LVLMs)来说是一个挑战。尽管这些模型可以描述视觉内容，但由于缺乏明确的定位，导致对象幻觉和不可靠的深度推理，限制了它们在无障碍指导方面的实用性。我们引入了WalkGPT，一种像素定位的LVLM，用于新的“定位导航指南”任务，它在一个单一架构中统一了语言推理和分割，以实现深度感知的无障碍指导。给定一个行人视角图像和一个导航查询，WalkGPT生成一个带有分割掩码的对话式响应，该掩码描绘了可访问和有害的特征，以及相对深度估计。该模型包含一个多尺度查询投影器(MSQP)，通过沿着文本标记跨空间层次聚合图像标记来塑造最终的图像标记，以及一个校准文本投影器(CTP)，在提出的区域对齐损失的指导下，将语言嵌入映射到分割感知的表示。这些组件实现了细粒度的定位和深度推断，无需用户提供的线索或锚点，从而使模型能够生成完整而真实的导航指导。我们还引入了PAVE，一个大规模基准测试数据集，包含4.1万张行人视角图像，以及与无障碍相关的提问和深度定位的答案。实验表明，WalkGPT实现了强大的定位推理和分割性能。源代码和数据集可在\href{https://sites.google.com/view/walkgpt-26/home}{项目网站}上找到。

title: World2Act: Latent Action Post-Training via Skill-Compositional World Models
summary: World Models (WMs) have emerged as a promising approach for post-training Vision-Language-Action (VLA) policies to improve robustness and generalization under environmental changes. However, most WM-based post-training methods rely on pixel-space supervision, making policies sensitive to pixel-level artifacts and hallucination from imperfect WM rollouts. We introduce World2Act, a post-training framework that aligns VLA actions directly with WM video-dynamics latents using a contrastive matching objective, reducing dependence on pixels. Post-training performance is tied to rollout quality, yet current WMs struggle with arbitrary-length video generation as they are mostly trained on fixed-length clips while robotic execution durations vary widely. To address this, we propose an automatic LLM-based skill-decomposition pipeline that segments high-level instructions into low-level prompts. Our pipeline produces RoboCasa-Skill and LIBERO-Skill, supporting skill-compositional WMs that remain temporally consistent across diverse task horizons. Empirically, applying World2Act to VLAs like GR00T-N1.6 and Cosmos Policy achieves state-of-the-art results on RoboCasa and LIBERO, and improves real-world performance by 6.7%, enhancing embodied agent generalization.
tldr: The paper introduces World2Act, a post-training framework aligning Vision-Language-Action policies with World Model latents and using a skill-decomposition pipeline for improved robotic execution and generalization across various task durations.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了World2Act，一个后训练框架，通过技能分解流水线将视觉-语言-动作策略与世界模型潜在空间对齐，以提高机器人执行能力和在不同任务时长下的泛化能力。
summary_zh: 世界模型（WMs）已经成为一种前景广阔的方法，用于对视觉-语言-动作（VLA）策略进行后训练，以提高其在环境变化下的鲁棒性和泛化能力。然而，大多数基于WM的后训练方法依赖于像素空间监督，这使得策略对像素级别的伪影以及不完善的WM推演产生的幻觉非常敏感。我们引入World2Act，一种后训练框架，它使用对比匹配目标将VLA动作直接与WM视频动态潜变量对齐，从而减少对像素的依赖。后训练性能与推演质量密切相关，但当前的WMs难以处理任意长度的视频生成，因为它们主要基于固定长度的片段进行训练，而机器人执行的时长变化很大。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种基于LLM的自动技能分解流程，将高层指令分割成低层提示。我们的流程生成了RoboCasa-Skill和LIBERO-Skill，支持技能组合式的WMs，从而在不同的任务范围内保持时间一致性。实验结果表明，将World2Act应用于GR00T-N1.6和Cosmos Policy等VLA策略，可以在RoboCasa和LIBERO上实现最先进的结果，并将现实世界性能提高了6.7%，从而增强了具身智能体的泛化能力。

title: DynVLA: Learning World Dynamics for Action Reasoning in Autonomous Driving
summary: We propose DynVLA, a driving VLA model that introduces a new CoT paradigm termed Dynamics CoT. DynVLA forecasts compact world dynamics before action generation, enabling more informed and physically grounded decision-making. To obtain compact dynamics representations, DynVLA introduces a Dynamics Tokenizer that compresses future evolution into a small set of dynamics tokens. Considering the rich environment dynamics in interaction-intensive driving scenarios, DynVLA decouples ego-centric and environment-centric dynamics, yielding more accurate world dynamics modeling. We then train DynVLA to generate dynamics tokens before actions through SFT and RFT, improving decision quality while maintaining latency-efficient inference. Compared to Textual CoT, which lacks fine-grained spatiotemporal understanding, and Visual CoT, which introduces substantial redundancy due to dense image prediction, Dynamics CoT captures the evolution of the world in a compact, interpretable, and efficient form. Extensive experiments on NAVSIM, Bench2Drive, and a large-scale in-house dataset demonstrate that DynVLA consistently outperforms Textual CoT and Visual CoT methods, validating the effectiveness and practical value of Dynamics CoT.
tldr: The paper introduces DynVLA, a driving VLA model that uses a Dynamics CoT approach to forecast compact world dynamics before action generation, improving decision-making in autonomous driving with better accuracy and efficiency compared to other CoT methods.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了DynVLA，一种驾驶VLA模型，它使用Dynamics CoT方法在动作生成之前预测紧凑的世界动态，与其它CoT方法相比，提高了自动驾驶中的决策能力，具有更好的准确性和效率。
summary_zh: 我们提出了DynVLA，一种驾驶VLA模型，引入了一种新的CoT范式，称为动态CoT。 DynVLA在动作生成之前预测紧凑的世界动力学，从而实现更明智和物理基础更扎实的决策。为了获得紧凑的动力学表示，DynVLA引入了一个动力学Tokenizer，将未来的演化压缩成一小组动力学令牌。考虑到交互密集型驾驶场景中丰富的环境动力学，DynVLA解耦了以自我为中心和以环境为中心的动力学，从而产生更准确的世界动力学建模。然后，我们通过SFT和RFT训练DynVLA在动作之前生成动力学令牌，从而在保持延迟高效推理的同时，提高决策质量。与缺乏细粒度时空理解的文本CoT相比，以及由于密集图像预测而引入大量冗余的视觉CoT相比，动态CoT以紧凑、可解释和高效的形式捕获世界的演化。在NAVSIM、Bench2Drive和一个大规模内部数据集上的大量实验表明，DynVLA始终优于文本CoT和视觉CoT方法，验证了动态CoT的有效性和实用价值。

title: PPGuide: Steering Diffusion Policies with Performance Predictive Guidance
summary: Diffusion policies have shown to be very efficient at learning complex, multi-modal behaviors for robotic manipulation. However, errors in generated action sequences can compound over time which can potentially lead to failure. Some approaches mitigate this by augmenting datasets with expert demonstrations or learning predictive world models which might be computationally expensive. We introduce Performance Predictive Guidance (PPGuide), a lightweight, classifier-based framework that steers a pre-trained diffusion policy away from failure modes at inference time. PPGuide makes use of a novel self-supervised process: it uses attention-based multiple instance learning to automatically estimate which observation-action chunks from the policy's rollouts are relevant to success or failure. We then train a performance predictor on this self-labeled data. During inference, this predictor provides a real-time gradient to guide the policy toward more robust actions. We validated our proposed PPGuide across a diverse set of tasks from the Robomimic and MimicGen benchmarks, demonstrating consistent improvements in performance.
tldr: The paper introduces PPGuide, a lightweight, classifier-based framework that uses self-supervised learning with attention-based multiple instance learning to steer pre-trained diffusion policies away from failure modes during robotic manipulation inference, showing improvements across diverse tasks.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为PPGuide的轻量级、基于分类器的框架，该框架利用基于注意力的多示例学习的自监督学习，在机器人操作推断过程中引导预训练的扩散策略远离失败模式，并在各种任务中表现出改进。
summary_zh: 扩散策略在学习用于机器人操作的复杂多模态行为方面表现出极高的效率。然而，生成动作序列中的误差会随着时间推移而累积，这可能导致失败。一些方法通过利用专家示范增强数据集或学习预测性世界模型来缓解这一问题，但这些方法可能计算成本很高。我们引入了性能预测引导（PPGuide），这是一个轻量级的、基于分类器的框架，可以在推理时引导预训练的扩散策略远离失败模式。PPGuide利用了一种新颖的自监督过程：它使用基于注意力的多示例学习自动估计策略rollout中哪些观察-动作片段与成功或失败相关。然后，我们基于这些自标记数据训练一个性能预测器。在推理过程中，该预测器提供实时梯度，以引导策略采取更稳健的动作。我们在来自Robomimic和MimicGen基准测试的一组多样化任务中验证了我们提出的PPGuide，证明了性能的持续改进。

title: Learning Adaptive Force Control for Contact-Rich Sample Scraping with Heterogeneous Materials
summary: The increasing demand for accelerated scientific discovery, driven by global challenges, highlights the need for advanced AI-driven robotics. Deploying robotic chemists in human-centric labs is key for the next horizon of autonomous discovery, as complex tasks still demand the dexterity of human scientists. Robotic manipulation in this context is uniquely challenged by handling diverse chemicals (granular, powdery, or viscous liquids), under varying lab conditions. For example, humans use spatulas for scraping materials from vial walls. Automating this process is challenging because it goes beyond simple robotic insertion tasks and traditional lab automation, requiring the execution of fine-granular movements within a constrained environment (the sample vial). Our work proposes an adaptive control framework to address this, relying on a low-level Cartesian impedance controller for stable and compliant physical interaction and a high-level reinforcement learning agent that learns to dynamically adjust interaction forces at the end-effector. The agent is guided by perception feedback, which provides the material's location. We first created a task-representative simulation environment with a Franka Research 3 robot, a scraping tool, and a sample vial containing heterogeneous materials. To facilitate the learning of an adaptive policy and model diverse characteristics, the sample is modelled as a collection of spheres, where each sphere is assigned a unique dislodgement force threshold, which is procedurally generated using Perlin noise. We train an agent to autonomously learn and adapt the optimal contact wrench for a sample scraping task in simulation and then successfully transfer this policy to a real robotic setup. Our method was evaluated across five different material setups, outperforming a fixed-wrench baseline by an average of 10.9%.
tldr: This paper presents a reinforcement learning framework for adaptive force control in a robotic sample scraping task with heterogeneous materials, demonstrating successful sim-to-real transfer and outperforming a fixed-wrench baseline.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种强化学习框架，用于机器人刮样任务中异质材料的自适应力控制。该方法成功实现了从仿真到真实的迁移，并优于固定力矩基线。
summary_zh: 在全球性挑战的驱动下，对加速科学发现的需求日益增长，凸显了对先进人工智能驱动机器人的需求。在以人为中心的实验室中部署机器人化学家是自主发现下一阶段的关键，因为复杂的任务仍然需要人类科学家的灵巧性。在这种背景下，机器人操作面临独特的挑战，需要在不同的实验室条件下处理各种化学物质（颗粒状、粉末状或粘性液体）。例如，人类使用刮刀从小瓶壁上刮取材料。自动化这一过程极具挑战性，因为它超越了简单的机器人插入任务和传统的实验室自动化，需要在受限环境（样品小瓶）内执行精细的运动。我们的工作提出了一种自适应控制框架来解决这个问题，该框架依赖于一个低层笛卡尔阻抗控制器来实现稳定和顺应性的物理交互，以及一个高层强化学习智能体，该智能体学习动态调整末端执行器的交互力。该智能体由感知反馈引导，感知反馈提供材料的位置。我们首先创建了一个具有代表性的任务仿真环境，该环境包含一个弗兰卡研究3型机器人、一个刮刀和一个装有异质材料的样品小瓶。为了促进自适应策略的学习并模拟多样化的特征，样品被建模为球体的集合，其中每个球体都被分配一个唯一的脱落力阈值，该阈值使用Perlin噪声程序化生成。我们训练了一个智能体来自主学习和调整样品刮取任务的最佳接触扭矩，并在仿真中，然后成功地将该策略转移到实际的机器人装置中。我们的方法在五种不同的材料设置中进行了评估，性能优于采用固定扭矩的基线，平均提高了10.9%。

title: Contact Coverage-Guided Exploration for General-Purpose Dexterous Manipulation
summary: Deep Reinforcement learning (DRL) has achieved remarkable success in domains with well-defined reward structures, such as Atari games and locomotion. In contrast, dexterous manipulation lacks general-purpose reward formulations and typically depends on task-specific, handcrafted priors to guide hand-object interactions. We propose Contact Coverage-Guided Exploration (CCGE), a general exploration method designed for general-purpose dexterous manipulation tasks. CCGE represents contact state as the intersection between object surface points and predefined hand keypoints, encouraging dexterous hands to discover diverse and novel contact patterns, namely which fingers contact which object regions. It maintains a contact counter conditioned on discretized object states obtained via learned hash codes, capturing how frequently each finger interacts with different object regions. This counter is leveraged in two complementary ways: (1) to assign a count-based contact coverage reward that promotes exploration of novel contact patterns, and (2) an energy-based reaching reward that guides the agent toward under-explored contact regions. We evaluate CCGE on a diverse set of dexterous manipulation tasks, including cluttered object singulation, constrained object retrieval, in-hand reorientation, and bimanual manipulation. Experimental results show that CCGE substantially improves training efficiency and success rates over existing exploration methods, and that the contact patterns learned with CCGE transfer robustly to real-world robotic systems. Project page is https://contact-coverage-guided-exploration.github.io.
tldr: The paper introduces Contact Coverage-Guided Exploration (CCGE), a novel reinforcement learning approach that guides exploration in dexterous manipulation by encouraging the discovery of diverse contact patterns between a robot hand and an object, improving training efficiency and enabling real-world transfer.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了接触覆盖引导探索（CCGE），一种新颖的强化学习方法，通过鼓励机器人手与物体之间发现不同的接触模式来指导灵巧操作中的探索，从而提高训练效率并实现现实世界的迁移。
summary_zh: 深度强化学习(DRL)在具有明确定义奖励结构的领域，如Atari游戏和运动控制，取得了显著成功。与之相对，灵巧操作缺乏通用的奖励函数，通常依赖于特定于任务的、手工制作的先验知识来指导手-物交互。我们提出了一种名为接触覆盖引导探索(CCGE)的通用探索方法，旨在用于通用的灵巧操作任务。CCGE将接触状态表示为物体表面点和预定义的手部关键点之间的交集，从而鼓励灵巧手发现多样化的和新颖的接触模式，即哪些手指接触物体的哪些区域。它维护一个基于离散化物体状态（通过学习的哈希码获得）的接触计数器，以捕获每个手指与不同物体区域交互的频率。该计数器以两种互补的方式使用：(1)分配一个基于计数的接触覆盖奖励，以促进对新颖接触模式的探索，以及(2)基于能量的到达奖励，引导智能体朝向未充分探索的接触区域。我们在各种灵巧操作任务上评估了CCGE，包括杂乱物体分离、约束物体检索、手内重定向和双手操作。实验结果表明，与现有的探索方法相比，CCGE显著提高了训练效率和成功率，并且使用CCGE学习到的接触模式可以稳健地转移到现实世界的机器人系统中。项目主页是https://contact-coverage-guided-exploration.github.io。

title: Lifelong Imitation Learning with Multimodal Latent Replay and Incremental Adjustment
summary: We introduce a lifelong imitation learning framework that enables continual policy refinement across sequential tasks under realistic memory and data constraints. Our approach departs from conventional experience replay by operating entirely in a multimodal latent space, where compact representations of visual, linguistic, and robot's state information are stored and reused to support future learning. To further stabilize adaptation, we introduce an incremental feature adjustment mechanism that regularizes the evolution of task embeddings through an angular margin constraint, preserving inter-task distinctiveness. Our method establishes a new state of the art in the LIBERO benchmarks, achieving 10-17 point gains in AUC and up to 65% less forgetting compared to previous leading methods. Ablation studies confirm the effectiveness of each component, showing consistent gains over alternative strategies. The code is available at: https://github.com/yfqi/lifelong_mlr_ifa.
tldr: This paper introduces a lifelong imitation learning framework using multimodal latent replay and incremental feature adjustment, achieving significant improvements in performance and reducing forgetting on the LIBERO benchmark.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种终身模仿学习框架，该框架使用多模态潜在重放和增量特征调整，在LIBERO基准测试中取得了显著的性能提升，并减少了遗忘。
summary_zh: 我们介绍了一种终身模仿学习框架，该框架能够在实际的内存和数据约束下，跨越连续任务进行持续的策略优化。我们的方法不同于传统的经验回放，它完全在多模态潜在空间中运作，视觉、语言和机器人状态信息的紧凑表示形式被存储和重用，以支持未来的学习。为了进一步稳定适应过程，我们引入了一种增量特征调整机制，该机制通过角度裕度约束来规范任务嵌入的演变，从而保持任务间的区分性。我们的方法在LIBERO基准测试中建立了新的最高水平，与之前领先的方法相比，在AUC中获得了10-17个点的提升，并将遗忘程度降低了最多65%。消融研究证实了每个组成部分的有效性，表明其相对于替代策略的一致增益。代码可在以下网址获取：https://github.com/yfqi/lifelong_mlr_ifa。

title: RL-Augmented MPC for Non-Gaited Legged and Hybrid Locomotion
summary: We propose a contact-explicit hierarchical architecture coupling Reinforcement Learning (RL) and Model Predictive Control (MPC), where a high-level RL agent provides gait and navigation commands to a low-level locomotion MPC. This offloads the combinatorial burden of contact timing from the MPC by learning acyclic gaits through trial and error in simulation. We show that only a minimal set of rewards and limited tuning are required to obtain effective policies. We validate the architecture in simulation across robotic platforms spanning 50 kg to 120 kg and different MPC implementations, observing the emergence of acyclic gaits and timing adaptations in flat-terrain legged and hybrid locomotion, and further demonstrating extensibility to non-flat terrains. Across all platforms, we achieve zero-shot sim-to-sim transfer without domain randomization, and we further demonstrate zero-shot sim-to-real transfer without domain randomization on Centauro, our 120 kg wheeled-legged humanoid robot. We make our software framework and evaluation results publicly available at https://github.com/AndrePatri/AugMPC.
tldr: This paper presents a hierarchical RL-MPC architecture for legged and hybrid locomotion, demonstrating successful zero-shot sim-to-sim and sim-to-real transfer on various robotic platforms without domain randomization.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种用于腿式和混合式运动的分层RL-MPC架构，展示了在各种机器人平台上成功的zero-shot sim-to-sim和sim-to-real迁移，无需领域随机化。
summary_zh: 我们提出了一种接触显式分层架构，将强化学习（RL）与模型预测控制（MPC）相结合，其中高层强化学习智能体向低层运动控制MPC提供步态和导航指令。这通过在仿真中试错学习无环步态，减轻了MPC的接触时序组合负担。我们表明，只需少量奖励和有限的调整即可获得有效的策略。我们在仿真中验证了该架构在50公斤到120公斤、采用不同MPC实现的多个机器人平台上的有效性，观察到在平坦地形的足式和混合运动中出现了无环步态和时序适应，并进一步证明了其对非平坦地形的扩展性。在所有平台中，我们实现了无需域随机化的零样本sim-to-sim迁移，并且我们进一步证明了无需域随机化的零样本sim-to-real迁移在我们的120公斤轮腿式人形机器人Centauro上的有效性。我们的软件框架和评估结果已公开，可在https://github.com/AndrePatri/AugMPC上获取。

title: FG-CLTP: Fine-Grained Contrastive Language Tactile Pretraining for Robotic Manipulation
summary: Recent advancements in integrating tactile sensing into vision-language-action (VLA) models have demonstrated transformative potential for robotic perception. However, existing tactile representations predominantly rely on qualitative descriptors (e.g., texture), neglecting quantitative contact states such as force magnitude, contact geometry, and principal axis orientation, which are indispensable for fine-grained manipulation. To bridge this gap, we propose FG-CLTP, a fine-grained contrastive language tactile pretraining framework. We first introduce a novel dataset comprising over 100k tactile 3D point cloud-language pairs that explicitly capture multidimensional contact states from the sensor's perspective. We then implement a discretized numerical tokenization mechanism to achieve quantitative-semantic alignment, effectively injecting explicit physical metrics into the multimodal feature space. The proposed FG-CLTP model yields a 95.9% classification accuracy and reduces the regression error (MAE) by 52.6% compared to state-of-the-art methods. Furthermore, the integration of 3D point cloud representations establishes a sensor-agnostic foundation with a minimal sim-to-real gap of 3.5%. Building upon this fine-grained representation, we develop a 3D tactile-language-action (3D-TLA) architecture driven by a flow matching policy to enable multimodal reasoning and control. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our framework significantly outperforms strong baselines in contact-rich manipulation tasks, providing a robust and generalizable foundation for tactile-language-action models.
tldr: The paper introduces FG-CLTP, a fine-grained contrastive language tactile pretraining framework using a novel tactile 3D point cloud-language dataset to improve robotic manipulation by incorporating quantitative contact states. It demonstrates superior performance in classification accuracy and regression error compared to existing methods.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了FG-CLTP，一种精细的对比语言触觉预训练框架，它使用了一种新型的触觉3D点云-语言数据集，通过整合定量接触状态来改善机器人操作。与现有方法相比，它在分类准确率和回归误差方面表现出卓越的性能。
summary_zh: 将触觉感知集成到视觉-语言-动作(VLA)模型中的最新进展，展示了机器人感知的变革潜力。 然而，现有的触觉表征主要依赖于定性描述符（例如，纹理），而忽略了定量接触状态，如力的大小、接触几何形状和主轴方向，这些对于细粒度操作是不可或缺的。 为了弥合这一差距，我们提出了FG-CLTP，一种细粒度对比语言触觉预训练框架。 我们首先引入了一个包含超过10万个触觉3D点云-语言对的新数据集，该数据集从传感器的角度明确捕捉了多维接触状态。 然后，我们实现了一种离散化数值标记机制，以实现定量-语义对齐，有效地将显式物理指标注入到多模态特征空间中。 相对于最先进的方法，所提出的FG-CLTP模型产生了95.9%的分类准确率，并将回归误差（MAE）降低了52.6%。 此外，3D点云表征的集成建立了一个传感器无关的基础，其sim-to-real差距最小，仅为3.5%。 在这种细粒度的表征基础上，我们开发了一种由流匹配策略驱动的3D触觉-语言-动作(3D-TLA)架构，以实现多模态推理和控制。 大量实验表明，我们的框架在富接触操作任务中显著优于强大的基线，为触觉-语言-动作模型提供了稳健且通用的基础。

title: ASTER: Attitude-aware Suspended-payload Quadrotor Traversal via Efficient Reinforcement Learning
summary: Agile maneuvering of the quadrotor cable-suspended system is significantly hindered by its non-smooth hybrid dynamics. While model-free Reinforcement Learning (RL) circumvents explicit differentiation of complex models, achieving attitude-constrained or inverted flight remains an open challenge due to the extreme reward sparsity under strict orientation requirements. This paper presents ASTER, a robust RL framework that achieves, to our knowledge, the first successful autonomous inverted flight for the cable-suspended system. We propose hybrid-dynamics-informed state seeding (HDSS), an initialization strategy that back-propagates target configurations through physics-consistent kinematic inversions across both taut and slack cable phases. HDSS enables the policy to discover aggressive maneuvers that are unreachable via standard exploration. Extensive simulations and real-world experiments demonstrate remarkable agility, precise attitude alignment, and robust zero-shot sim-to-real transfer across complex trajectories.
tldr: This paper introduces ASTER, a reinforcement learning framework that achieves the first successful autonomous inverted flight for cable-suspended quadrotor systems via a hybrid-dynamics-informed state seeding strategy, demonstrating robust sim-to-real transfer.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了ASTER，一个强化学习框架，通过混合动力学信息的状态初始化策略，首次成功实现了缆绳悬挂四旋翼飞行器的自主倒飞，并展示了强大的从仿真到现实的迁移能力。
summary_zh: 四旋翼绳索悬挂系统的敏捷机动显著受到其非光滑混合动力学特性的限制。虽然无模型强化学习（RL）规避了对复杂模型的显式微分，但由于在严格姿态要求下的极端奖励稀疏性，实现姿态约束或倒飞仍然是一个公开的挑战。本文提出ASTER，一个鲁棒的RL框架，据我们所知，它首次成功实现了绳索悬挂系统的自主倒飞。我们提出了混合动力学信息状态播种（HDSS），一种初始化策略，该策略通过物理一致的运动学逆解，跨越张紧和松弛两种绳索状态，反向传播目标配置。HDSS使得策略能够发现通过标准探索无法达到的激进机动。广泛的仿真和真实世界的实验表明，ASTER具有卓越的敏捷性、精确的姿态对准以及鲁棒的零样本从仿真到现实的迁移能力，适用于复杂的轨迹。

title: MAVEN: A Meta-Reinforcement Learning Framework for Varying-Dynamics Expertise in Agile Quadrotor Maneuvers
summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for achieving online agile navigation with quadrotors. Despite this success, policies trained via standard RL typically fail to generalize across significant dynamic variations, exhibiting a critical lack of adaptability. This work introduces MAVEN, a meta-RL framework that enables a single policy to achieve robust end-to-end navigation across a wide range of quadrotor dynamics. Our approach features a novel predictive context encoder, which learns to infer a latent representation of the system dynamics from interaction history. We demonstrate our method in agile waypoint traversal tasks under two challenging scenarios: large variations in quadrotor mass and severe single-rotor thrust loss. We leverage a GPU-vectorized simulator to distribute tasks across thousands of parallel environments, overcoming the long training times of meta-RL to converge in less than an hour. Through extensive experiments in both simulation and the real world, we validate that MAVEN achieves superior adaptation and agility. The policy successfully executes zero-shot sim-to-real transfer, demonstrating robust online adaptation by performing high-speed maneuvers despite mass variations of up to 66.7% and single-rotor thrust losses as severe as 70%.
tldr: The paper introduces MAVEN, a meta-RL framework with a predictive context encoder, enabling agile quadrotor navigation across significant dynamic variations, validated through sim-to-real transfer.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了MAVEN，一个带有预测上下文编码器的元强化学习框架，能够实现四旋翼飞行器在显著动态变化下的敏捷导航，并通过仿真到真实的迁移进行了验证。
summary_zh: 强化学习(RL)已成为实现四旋翼飞行器在线敏捷导航的强大范例。尽管取得了成功，但通过标准RL训练的策略通常无法在显著的动态变化中泛化，表现出关键的适应性不足。 本文提出了MAVEN，一个元强化学习框架，使单个策略能够在各种四旋翼飞行器动态下实现鲁棒的端到端导航。 我们的方法采用了一种新颖的预测上下文编码器，该编码器学习从交互历史中推断系统动态的潜在表示。 我们在两种具有挑战性的场景下，即四旋翼飞行器质量的大幅变化和严重的单旋翼推力损失，展示了我们的方法在敏捷航点遍历任务中的应用。 我们利用GPU矢量化模拟器将任务分配到数千个并行环境中，克服了元强化学习的漫长训练时间，从而在不到一小时内收敛。 通过在仿真和现实世界中的大量实验，我们验证了MAVEN实现了卓越的适应性和敏捷性。 该策略成功执行了零样本的仿真到现实的迁移，通过执行高速机动，即使在高达66.7%的质量变化和高达70%的单旋翼推力损失的情况下，也展示了强大的在线适应能力。

title: FutureVLA: Joint Visuomotor Prediction for Vision-Language-Action Model
summary: Predictive foresight is important to intelligent embodied agents. Since the motor execution of a robot is intrinsically constrained by its visual perception of environmental geometry, effectively anticipating the future requires capturing this tightly coupled visuomotor interplay. While recent vision-language-action models attempt to incorporate future guidance, they struggle with this joint modeling. Existing explicit methods divert capacity to task-irrelevant visual details, whereas implicit methods relying on sparse frame pairs disrupt temporal continuity. By heavily relying on visual reconstruction, these methods become visually dominated, entangling static scene context with dynamic action intent. We argue that effective joint visuomotor predictive modeling requires both temporal continuity and visually-conditioned supervision decoupling. To this end, we propose FutureVLA, featuring a novel Joint Visuomotor Predictive Architecture. FutureVLA is designed to extract joint visuomotor embeddings by first decoupling visual and motor information, and then jointly encoding generalized physical priors. Specifically, in the pretraining stage, we leverage heterogeneous manipulation datasets and introduce a Joint Visuomotor Gating mechanism to structurally separate visual state preservation from temporal action modeling. It allows the motor stream to focus on continuous physical dynamics while explicitly querying visual tokens for environmental constraints, yielding highly generalizable joint visuomotor embeddings. Subsequently, in the post-training stage, we employ a latent embeddings alignment strategy, enabling diverse downstream VLA models to internalize these temporal priors without modifying their inference architectures. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FutureVLA consistently improves VLA frameworks.
tldr: The paper introduces FutureVLA, a novel architecture for joint visuomotor prediction in vision-language-action models. It uses a joint visuomotor gating mechanism during pretraining to decouple visual state preservation from temporal action modeling, leading to improved VLA performance.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了FutureVLA，一种用于视觉-语言-动作模型中联合视觉运动预测的新架构。它在预训练阶段使用联合视觉运动门控机制来分离视觉状态保持和时间动作建模，从而提高VLA性能。
summary_zh: 预测性前瞻对于智能具身智能体至关重要。由于机器人的运动执行本质上受到其对环境几何视觉感知的制约，有效预测未来需要捕捉这种紧密耦合的视觉运动交互。虽然最近的视觉-语言-动作模型试图纳入未来指导，但它们在联合建模方面存在困难。现有的显式方法将能力转移到与任务无关的视觉细节上，而依赖于稀疏帧对的隐式方法则破坏了时间连续性。通过过度依赖视觉重建，这些方法变得视觉主导，将静态场景上下文与动态动作意图纠缠在一起。我们认为，有效的联合视觉运动预测建模需要时间连续性和视觉条件化的监督解耦。为此，我们提出了一种新的联合视觉运动预测架构FutureVLA。 FutureVLA旨在通过首先解耦视觉和运动信息，然后联合编码广义物理先验来提取联合视觉运动嵌入。具体来说，在预训练阶段，我们利用异构操作数据集，并引入联合视觉运动门控机制，以在结构上将视觉状态保持与时间动作建模分离。它允许运动流专注于连续物理动力学，同时显式查询视觉标记的环境约束，从而产生高度通用的联合视觉运动嵌入。随后，在后训练阶段，我们采用潜在嵌入对齐策略，使各种下游 VLA 模型无需修改其推理架构即可内化这些时间先验。大量实验表明，FutureVLA 能够持续改进 VLA 框架。

title: Cybo-Waiter: A Physical Agentic Framework for Humanoid Whole-Body Locomotion-Manipulation
summary: Robots are increasingly expected to execute open ended natural language requests in human environments, which demands reliable long horizon execution under partial observability. This is especially challenging for humanoids because locomotion and manipulation are tightly coupled through stance, reachability, and balance. We present a humanoid agent framework that turns VLM plans into verifiable task programs and closes the loop with multi object 3D geometric supervision. A VLM planner compiles each instruction into a typed JSON sequence of subtasks with explicit predicate based preconditions and success conditions. Using SAM3 and RGB-D, we ground all task relevant entities in 3D, estimate object centroids and extents, and evaluate predicates over stable frames to obtain condition level diagnostics. The supervisor uses these diagnostics to verify subtask completion and to provide condition-level feedback for progression and replanning. We execute each subtask by coordinating humanoid locomotion and whole-body manipulation, selecting feasible motion primitives under reachability and balance constraints. Experiments on tabletop manipulation and long horizon humanoid loco manipulation tasks show improved robustness from multi object grounding, temporal stability, and recovery driven replanning.
tldr: The paper introduces Cybo-Waiter, a framework for humanoid robots to execute natural language requests by integrating VLM planning with 3D geometric supervision for robust locomotion-manipulation, showing improved performance in tabletop and long-horizon tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了Cybo-Waiter，一个通过结合VLM规划和3D几何监督使人形机器人执行自然语言请求的框架，实现了稳健的运动操作，并在桌面和长时程任务中表现出改进的性能。
summary_zh: 机器人越来越需要在人类环境中执行开放式的自然语言请求，这要求在部分可观测条件下进行可靠的长时程执行。对于人形机器人来说，这一点尤其具有挑战性，因为运动和操作通过姿态、可达性和平衡紧密耦合。我们提出了一个人形机器人代理框架，该框架将VLM计划转化为可验证的任务程序，并通过多目标3D几何监督闭环。VLM规划器将每条指令编译成一个类型化的JSON子任务序列，其中包含基于谓词的显式先决条件和成功条件。利用SAM3和RGB-D数据，我们在3D空间中定位所有与任务相关的实体，估计物体质心和范围，并评估稳定帧上的谓词，以获得条件级别的诊断信息。监督器使用这些诊断信息来验证子任务的完成情况，并提供条件级别的反馈以进行推进和重新规划。我们通过协调人形机器人的运动和全身操作来执行每个子任务，并在可达性和平衡约束下选择可行的运动基元。在桌面操作和长时程人形机器人运动操作任务上的实验表明，多目标定位、时间稳定性以及由恢复驱动的重新规划提高了鲁棒性。

title: AdaClearGrasp: Learning Adaptive Clearing for Zero-Shot Robust Dexterous Grasping in Densely Cluttered Environments
summary: In densely cluttered environments, physical interference, visual occlusions, and unstable contacts often cause direct dexterous grasping to fail, while aggressive singulation strategies may compromise safety. Enabling robots to adaptively decide whether to clear surrounding objects or directly grasp the target is therefore crucial for robust manipulation. We propose AdaClearGrasp, a closed-loop decision-execution framework for adaptive clearing and zero-shot dexterous grasping in densely cluttered environments. The framework formulates manipulation as a controllable high-level decision process that determines whether to directly grasp the target or first clear surrounding objects. A pretrained vision-language model (VLM) interprets visual observations and language task descriptions to reason about grasp interference and generate a high-level planning skeleton, which invokes structured atomic skills through a unified action interface. For dexterous grasping, we train a reinforcement learning policy with a relative hand-object distance representation, enabling zero-shot generalization across diverse object geometries and physical properties. During execution, visual feedback monitors outcomes and triggers replanning upon failures, forming a closed-loop correction mechanism. To evaluate language-conditioned dexterous grasping in clutter, we introduce Clutter-Bench, the first simulation benchmark with graded clutter complexity. It includes seven target objects across three clutter levels, yielding 210 task scenarios. We further perform sim-to-real experiments on three objects under three clutter levels (18 scenarios). Results demonstrate that AdaClearGrasp significantly improves grasp success rates in densely cluttered environments. For more videos and code, please visit our project website: https://chenzixuan99.github.io/adaclear-grasp.github.io/.
tldr: The paper introduces AdaClearGrasp, a framework that uses a vision-language model and reinforcement learning to adaptively clear clutter before grasping objects in densely cluttered environments, demonstrating improved success rates in simulated and real-world experiments.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 AdaClearGrasp，一个使用视觉语言模型和强化学习的框架，能够自适应地清除杂乱环境中的障碍物，然后再抓取物体，在模拟和真实世界的实验中都展示了更高的成功率。
summary_zh: 在密集杂乱环境中，物理干涉、视觉遮挡和不稳定的接触通常会导致直接灵巧抓取失败，而激进的分离策略可能会损害安全性。 因此，使机器人能够自适应地决定是否清除周围物体或直接抓取目标对于鲁棒操作至关重要。 我们提出了 AdaClearGrasp，这是一个闭环决策-执行框架，用于在密集杂乱环境中进行自适应清除和零样本灵巧抓取。 该框架将操作制定为一个可控的高级决策过程，该过程确定是直接抓取目标还是首先清除周围物体。 预训练的视觉语言模型 (VLM) 解释视觉观察和语言任务描述，以推理抓取干涉并生成高级规划骨架，该骨架通过统一的动作接口调用结构化的原子技能。 对于灵巧抓取，我们使用相对手-物距离表示训练了一个强化学习策略，从而能够在不同的物体几何形状和物理属性上进行零样本泛化。 在执行过程中，视觉反馈会监控结果并在失败时触发重新规划，从而形成闭环校正机制。 为了评估杂乱环境中的语言条件灵巧抓取，我们引入了 Clutter-Bench，这是第一个具有分级杂乱复杂性的仿真基准。 它包括跨越三个杂乱级别的七个目标物体，产生 210 个任务场景。 我们进一步在三个杂乱级别下的三个物体上进行了从仿真到真实的实验（18 个场景）。 结果表明，AdaClearGrasp 显著提高了在密集杂乱环境中的抓取成功率。 更多视频和代码，请访问我们的项目网站：https://chenzixuan99.github.io/adaclear-grasp.github.io/。

title: RC-NF: Robot-Conditioned Normalizing Flow for Real-Time Anomaly Detection in Robotic Manipulation
summary: Recent advances in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have enabled robots to execute increasingly complex tasks. However, VLA models trained through imitation learning struggle to operate reliably in dynamic environments and often fail under Out-of-Distribution (OOD) conditions. To address this issue, we propose Robot-Conditioned Normalizing Flow (RC-NF), a real-time monitoring model for robotic anomaly detection and intervention that ensures the robot's state and the object's motion trajectory align with the task. RC-NF decouples the processing of task-aware robot and object states within the normalizing flow. It requires only positive samples for unsupervised training and calculates accurate robotic anomaly scores during inference through the probability density function. We further present LIBERO-Anomaly-10, a benchmark comprising three categories of robotic anomalies for simulation evaluation. RC-NF achieves state-of-the-art performance across all anomaly types compared to previous methods in monitoring robotic tasks. Real-world experiments demonstrate that RC-NF operates as a plug-and-play module for VLA models (e.g., pi0), providing a real-time OOD signal that enables state-level rollback or task-level replanning when necessary, with a response latency under 100 ms. These results demonstrate that RC-NF noticeably enhances the robustness and adaptability of VLA-based robotic systems in dynamic environments.
tldr: The paper introduces RC-NF, a robot-conditioned normalizing flow for real-time anomaly detection in robotic manipulation, which enhances the robustness of VLA models in dynamic environments by providing a real-time OOD signal for rollback or replanning.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种机器人条件归一化流 (RC-NF)，用于机器人操作中的实时异常检测，通过为回滚或重规划提供实时 OOD 信号，从而增强 VLA 模型在动态环境中的鲁棒性。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型的最新进展使得机器人能够执行日益复杂的任务。然而，通过模仿学习训练的VLA模型在动态环境中可靠运行方面存在困难，并且经常在分布外（OOD）条件下失效。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种机器人条件归一化流（RC-NF），这是一种用于机器人异常检测和干预的实时监控模型，它确保机器人的状态和物体的运动轨迹与任务对齐。RC-NF在归一化流中解耦了任务相关的机器人和物体状态的处理。它只需要正样本进行无监督训练，并通过概率密度函数在推理过程中计算准确的机器人异常分数。我们进一步提出了LIBERO-Anomaly-10，这是一个由三种机器人异常类别组成的基准，用于模拟评估。与之前的方法相比，RC-NF在监控机器人任务的所有异常类型中都取得了最先进的性能。真实世界实验表明，RC-NF可以作为VLA模型（例如，pi0）的即插即用模块运行，提供实时OOD信号，从而在必要时实现状态级别的回滚或任务级别的重新规划，响应延迟低于100毫秒。这些结果表明，RC-NF显著增强了基于VLA的机器人系统在动态环境中的鲁棒性和适应性。

title: Thousand-GPU Large-Scale Training and Optimization Recipe for AI-Native Cloud Embodied Intelligence Infrastructure
summary: Embodied intelligence is a key step towards Artificial General Intelligence (AGI), yet its development faces multiple challenges including data, frameworks, infrastructure, and evaluation systems. To address these issues, we have, for the first time in the industry, launched a cloud-based, thousand-GPU distributed training platform for embodied intelligence, built upon the widely adopted LeRobot framework, and have systematically overcome bottlenecks across the entire pipeline. At the data layer, we have restructured the data pipeline to optimize the flow of embodied training data. In terms of training, for the GR00T-N1.5 model, utilizing thousand-GPU clusters and data at the scale of hundreds of millions, the single-round training time has been reduced from 15 hours to just 22 minutes, achieving a 40-fold speedup. At the model layer, by combining variable-length FlashAttention and Data Packing, we have moved from sample redundancy to sequence integration, resulting in a 188% speed increase; π-0.5 attention optimization has accelerated training by 165%; and FP8 quantization has delivered a 140% speedup. On the infrastructure side, relying on high-performance storage, a 3.2T RDMA network, and a Ray-driven elastic AI data lake, we have achieved deep synergy among data, storage, communication, and computation. We have also built an end-to-end evaluation system, creating a closed loop from training to simulation to assessment. This framework has already been fully validated on thousand-GPU clusters, laying a crucial technical foundation for the development and application of next-generation autonomous intelligent robots, and is expected to accelerate the arrival of the era of human-machine integration.
tldr: This paper presents a cloud-based, thousand-GPU distributed training platform for embodied intelligence, achieving significant speedups through data pipeline optimization, model layer improvements (FlashAttention, Data Packing, Attention Optimization, and FP8 quantization), and infrastructure enhancements. They claim to have built an end-to-end evaluation system validated on thousand-GPU clusters.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一个基于云的、千GPU分布式具身智能训练平台，通过数据管道优化、模型层改进（FlashAttention、数据压缩、注意力优化和FP8量化）以及基础设施增强，实现了显著的加速。他们声称构建了一个在千GPU集群上验证过的端到端评估系统。
summary_zh: 具身智能是通往通用人工智能(AGI)的关键一步，但其发展面临着数据、框架、基础设施和评估系统等多重挑战。为了解决这些问题，我们在业界首次推出了基于云端的、千GPU分布式具身智能训练平台，该平台建立在广泛使用的LeRobot框架之上，并系统性地克服了整个流程中的瓶颈。在数据层，我们重构了数据管道，以优化具身训练数据的流程。在训练方面，对于GR00T-N1.5模型，利用千GPU集群和数亿级别的数据，单轮训练时间从15小时缩短到仅22分钟，实现了40倍的加速。在模型层，通过结合变长FlashAttention和数据打包，我们从样本冗余转向序列集成，从而实现了188%的速度提升；π-0.5注意力优化加速了165%的训练；FP8量化则带来了140%的加速。在基础设施方面，依托高性能存储、3.2T RDMA网络和Ray驱动的弹性AI数据湖，我们实现了数据、存储、通信和计算之间的深度协同。我们还构建了一个端到端的评估系统，形成从训练到仿真再到评估的闭环。该框架已在千GPU集群上得到充分验证，为下一代自主智能机器人的开发和应用奠定了关键的技术基础，并有望加速人机融合时代的到来。

title: FAR-Dex: Few-shot Data Augmentation and Adaptive Residual Policy Refinement for Dexterous Manipulation
summary: Achieving human-like dexterous manipulation through the collaboration of multi-fingered hands with robotic arms remains a longstanding challenge in robotics, primarily due to the scarcity of high-quality demonstrations and the complexity of high-dimensional action spaces. To address these challenges, we propose FAR-Dex, a hierarchical framework that integrates few-shot data augmentation with adaptive residual refinement to enable robust and precise arm-hand coordination in dexterous tasks. First, FAR-DexGen leverages the IsaacLab simulator to generate diverse and physically constrained trajectories from a few demonstrations, providing a data foundation for policy training. Second, FAR-DexRes introduces an adaptive residual module that refines policies by combining multi-step trajectory segments with observation features, thereby enhancing accuracy and robustness in manipulation scenarios. Experiments in both simulation and real-world demonstrate that FAR-Dex improves data quality by 13.4% and task success rates by 7% over state-of-the-art methods. It further achieves over 80% success in real-world tasks, enabling fine-grained dexterous manipulation with strong positional generalization.
tldr: The paper introduces FAR-Dex, a hierarchical framework combining few-shot data augmentation and adaptive residual refinement for robust and precise arm-hand coordination in dexterous manipulation, showing improvements in data quality and task success rates both in simulation and real-world experiments.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 FAR-Dex，一个结合了少量数据增强和自适应残差细化的分层框架，用于在灵巧操作中实现鲁棒和精确的臂手协调。实验结果表明，该方法在仿真和真实世界中均提高了数据质量和任务成功率。
summary_zh: 通过多指灵巧手与机械臂的协同配合来实现类人灵巧操作一直是机器人领域的一项长期挑战，这主要是由于高质量演示数据的稀缺以及高维动作空间的复杂性。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出FAR-Dex，一个分层框架，它集成了少样本数据增强与自适应残差优化，以实现在灵巧任务中稳健而精确的臂-手协调。首先，FAR-DexGen 利用 IsaacLab 模拟器，从少量演示数据生成多样且受物理约束的轨迹，为策略训练提供数据基础。其次，FAR-DexRes 引入了一个自适应残差模块，通过将多步轨迹片段与观测特征相结合来优化策略，从而提高操作场景中的精度和鲁棒性。在仿真和真实世界中的实验表明，FAR-Dex 将数据质量提高了 13.4%，任务成功率比最先进的方法提高了 7%。它还在真实世界任务中实现了超过 80% 的成功率，从而实现了具有强大位置泛化能力的精细灵巧操作。

title: AsyncMDE: Real-Time Monocular Depth Estimation via Asynchronous Spatial Memory
summary: Foundation-model-based monocular depth estimation offers a viable alternative to active sensors for robot perception, yet its computational cost often prohibits deployment on edge platforms. Existing methods perform independent per-frame inference, wasting the substantial computational redundancy between adjacent viewpoints in continuous robot operation. This paper presents AsyncMDE, an asynchronous depth perception system consisting of a foundation model and a lightweight model that amortizes the foundation model's computational cost over time. The foundation model produces high-quality spatial features in the background, while the lightweight model runs asynchronously in the foreground, fusing cached memory with current observations through complementary fusion, outputting depth estimates, and autoregressively updating the memory. This enables cross-frame feature reuse with bounded accuracy degradation. At a mere 3.83M parameters, it operates at 237 FPS on an RTX 4090, recovering 77% of the accuracy gap to the foundation model while achieving a 25X parameter reduction. Validated across indoor static, dynamic, and synthetic extreme-motion benchmarks, AsyncMDE degrades gracefully between refreshes and achieves 161FPS on a Jetson AGX Orin with TensorRT, clearly demonstrating its feasibility for real-time edge deployment.
tldr: AsyncMDE presents an asynchronous monocular depth estimation system that uses a foundation model and a lightweight model to achieve real-time performance on edge devices by reusing features across frames, significantly reducing computational cost while maintaining reasonable accuracy.
tldr_zh: AsyncMDE提出了一种异步单目深度估计系统，该系统利用基础模型和轻量级模型，通过重用跨帧特征在边缘设备上实现实时性能，显著降低了计算成本，同时保持了合理的精度。
summary_zh: 基于基础模型的单目深度估计为机器人感知提供了一种可行的替代主动传感器的方案，但其计算成本通常限制了在边缘平台上的部署。现有方法执行独立的逐帧推理，浪费了连续机器人操作中相邻视点之间的大量计算冗余。本文提出了一种异步深度感知系统AsyncMDE，它由一个基础模型和一个轻量级模型组成，以分摊基础模型随时间的计算成本。基础模型在后台生成高质量的空间特征，而轻量级模型在前台异步运行，通过互补融合将缓存的记忆与当前观测融合，输出深度估计值，并自回归地更新记忆。这实现了跨帧特征重用，并具有有界的精度退化。仅需383万参数，它在RTX 4090上以237 FPS运行，恢复了基础模型77%的精度损失，同时实现了25倍的参数减少。在室内静态、动态和合成极端运动基准测试中验证表明，AsyncMDE在刷新间隔期间性能平稳退化，并在带有TensorRT的Jetson AGX Orin上实现了161FPS，清楚地表明了其在实时边缘部署中的可行性。

title: COHORT: Hybrid RL for Collaborative Large DNN Inference on Multi-Robot Systems Under Real-Time Constraints
summary: Large deep neural networks (DNNs), especially transformer-based and multimodal architectures, are computationally demanding and challenging to deploy on resource-constrained edge platforms like field robots. These challenges intensify in mission-critical scenarios (e.g., disaster response), where robots must collaborate under tight constraints on bandwidth, latency, and battery life, often without infrastructure or server support. To address these limitations, we present COHORT, a collaborative DNN inference and task-execution framework for multi-robot systems built on the Robotic Operating System (ROS). COHORT employs a hybrid offline-online reinforcement learning (RL) strategy to dynamically schedule and distribute DNN module execution across robots. Our key contributions are threefold: (a) Offline RL policy learning combined with Advantage-Weighted Regression (AWR), trained on auction-based task allocation data from heterogeneous DNN workloads across distributed robots, (b) Online policy adaptation via Multi-Agent PPO (MAPPO), initialized from the offline policy and fine-tuned in real time, and (c) comprehensive evaluation of COHORT on vision-language model (VLM) inference tasks such as CLIP and SAM, analyzing scalability with increasing robot/workload and robustness under . We benchmark COHORT against genetic algorithms and multiple RL baselines. Experimental results demonstrate that COHORT reduces battery consumption by 15.4% and increases GPU utilization by 51.67%, while satisfying frame-rate and deadline constraints 2.55 times of the time.
tldr: The paper presents COHORT, a hybrid offline-online RL framework for collaborative DNN inference on multi-robot systems, achieving improved battery consumption, GPU utilization, and deadline satisfaction compared to baselines.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了COHORT，一个混合离线-在线强化学习框架，用于多机器人系统上的协同DNN推理，与基线相比，提高了电池消耗、GPU利用率和截止时间满意度。
summary_zh: 大型深度神经网络（DNN），尤其是基于Transformer和多模态架构的网络，计算需求高且难以在资源受限的边缘平台（如现场机器人）上部署。在任务关键型场景（例如灾难响应）中，这些挑战会加剧，在这些场景中，机器人必须在带宽、延迟和电池寿命的严格约束下进行协作，并且通常没有基础设施或服务器支持。为了解决这些限制，我们提出了COHORT，一个基于机器人操作系统（ROS）的多机器人系统协作式DNN推理和任务执行框架。COHORT采用混合离线-在线强化学习（RL）策略，以动态地调度和分配DNN模块在机器人之间的执行。我们的主要贡献有三个方面：（a）离线RL策略学习与优势加权回归（AWR）相结合，在分布式机器人上，利用来自异构DNN工作负载的基于拍卖的任务分配数据进行训练，（b）通过多智能体PPO（MAPPO）进行在线策略自适应，从离线策略初始化并在实时环境中微调，（c）在视觉-语言模型（VLM）推理任务（如CLIP和SAM）上对COHORT进行全面评估，分析了随机器人/工作负载增加的可扩展性以及在情况下的鲁棒性。我们以遗传算法和多个RL基线作为基准来评估COHORT。实验结果表明，COHORT降低了15.4%的电池消耗，并提高了51.67%的GPU利用率，同时满足帧率和截止时间约束的时间是2.55倍。

title: Safe Probabilistic Planning for Human-Robot Interaction using Conformal Risk Control
summary: In this paper, we present a novel probabilistic safe control framework for human-robot interaction that combines control barrier functions (CBFs) with conformal risk control to provide formal safety guarantees while considering complex human behavior. The approach uses conformal risk control to quantify and control the prediction errors in CBF safety values and establishes formal guarantees on the probability of constraint satisfaction during interaction. We introduce an algorithm that dynamically adjusts the safety margins produced by conformal risk control based on the current interaction context. Through experiments on human-robot navigation scenarios, we demonstrate that our approach significantly reduces collision rates and safety violations as compared to baseline methods while maintaining high success rates in goal-reaching tasks and efficient control. The code, simulations, and other supplementary material can be found on the project website: https://jakeagonzales.github.io/crc-cbf-website/.
tldr: This paper introduces a novel safe control framework for human-robot interaction using control barrier functions (CBFs) with conformal risk control, demonstrating reduced collision rates and safety violations while maintaining task success.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种新颖的用于人机交互的安全控制框架，该框架结合了控制障碍函数（CBF）和共形风险控制，在保证任务成功的同时，降低了碰撞率和安全违规。
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种新型的概率安全控制框架，用于人机交互，该框架结合了控制屏障函数（CBFs）和共形风险控制，在考虑复杂人类行为的同时，提供形式化的安全保障。该方法利用共形风险控制来量化和控制CBF安全值中的预测误差，并建立交互过程中约束满足概率的形式化保证。我们引入了一种算法，该算法根据当前的交互环境动态调整由共形风险控制产生的安全裕度。通过在人机导航场景中的实验，我们证明了与基线方法相比，我们的方法显著降低了碰撞率和安全违规率，同时保持了在目标到达任务中的高成功率和高效控制。代码、模拟和其他补充材料可以在项目网站上找到：https://jakeagonzales.github.io/crc-cbf-website/.

title: ScanDP: Generalizable 3D Scanning with Diffusion Policy
summary: Learning-based 3D Scanning plays a crucial role in enabling efficient and accurate scanning of target objects. However, recent reinforcement learning-based methods often require large-scale training data and still struggle to generalize to unseen object categories.In this work, we propose a data-efficient 3D scanning framework that uses Diffusion Policy to imitate human-like scanning strategies. To enhance robustness and generalization, we adopt the Occupancy Grid Mapping instead of direct point cloud processing, offering improved noise resilience and handling of diverse object geometries. We also introduce a hybrid approach combining a sphere-based space representation with a path optimization procedure that ensures path safety and scanning efficiency. This approach addresses limitations in conventional imitation learning, such as redundant or unpredictable behavior. We evaluate our method on diverse unseen objects in both shape and scale. Ours achieves higher coverage and shorter paths than baselines, while remaining robust to sensor noise. We further confirm practical feasibility and stable operation in real-world execution.
tldr: This paper introduces ScanDP, a data-efficient 3D scanning framework using Diffusion Policy to imitate human-like scanning, improving generalization and robustness on unseen objects via Occupancy Grid Mapping and a hybrid space representation.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为 ScanDP 的数据高效 3D 扫描框架，它使用扩散策略模仿人类的扫描方式，并通过占用网格映射和混合空间表示来提高对未见物体的泛化能力和鲁棒性。
summary_zh: 基于学习的3D扫描在实现对目标物体高效且精确的扫描方面起着至关重要的作用。然而，最近基于强化学习的方法通常需要大规模的训练数据，并且仍然难以泛化到未见过的物体类别。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种数据高效的3D扫描框架，该框架使用扩散策略来模仿类人的扫描策略。为了增强鲁棒性和泛化性，我们采用占用栅格地图来代替直接的点云处理，从而提高了抗噪声能力并能处理多种多样的物体几何结构。我们还引入了一种混合方法，将基于球体的空间表示与路径优化程序相结合，以确保路径安全性和扫描效率。此方法解决了传统模仿学习中的局限性，例如冗余或不可预测的行为。我们在形状和尺度上都不同的未见物体的多样性上评估了我们的方法。我们的方法比基线方法实现了更高的覆盖率和更短的路径，同时对传感器噪声保持鲁棒性。我们进一步在真实世界的执行中证实了其实用可行性和稳定运行。

title: Update-Free On-Policy Steering via Verifiers
summary: In recent years, Behavior Cloning (BC) has become one of the most prevalent methods for enabling robots to mimic human demonstrations. However, despite their successes, BC policies are often brittle and struggle with precise manipulation. To overcome these issues, we propose UF-OPS, an Update-Free On-Policy Steering method that enables the robot to predict the success likelihood of its actions and adapt its strategy at execution time. We accomplish this by training verifier functions using policy rollout data obtained during an initial evaluation of the policy. These verifiers are subsequently used to steer the base policy toward actions with a higher likelihood of success. Our method improves the performance of black-box diffusion policy, without changing the base parameters, making it light-weight and flexible. We present results from both simulation and real-world data and achieve an average 49% improvement in success rate over the base policy across 5 real tasks.
tldr: The paper proposes UF-OPS, an update-free method using verifier functions to steer a black-box policy towards more successful actions, achieving a 49% success rate improvement on real-world robotic tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种名为UF-OPS的免更新方法，使用验证器函数引导黑盒策略执行更成功的动作，在真实机器人任务中成功率提升了49%。
summary_zh: 近年来，行为克隆（BC）已成为使机器人模仿人类演示的最流行方法之一。然而，尽管取得了成功，行为克隆策略通常是脆弱的，并且难以进行精确操作。为了克服这些问题，我们提出了一种无需更新的在线策略引导方法（UF-OPS），该方法使机器人能够预测其动作的成功可能性，并在执行时调整其策略。我们通过使用在策略的初始评估期间获得的策略 rollout 数据来训练验证器函数来实现这一点。随后，这些验证器用于引导基础策略朝着具有更高成功可能性的动作发展。我们的方法在不改变基础参数的情况下提高了黑盒扩散策略的性能，使其轻量且灵活。我们展示了来自模拟和真实世界数据的实验结果，并在5个真实任务中实现了比基础策略平均49%的成功率提升。

title: From Prior to Pro: Efficient Skill Mastery via Distribution Contractive RL Finetuning
summary: We introduce Distribution Contractive Reinforcement Learning (DICE-RL), a framework that uses reinforcement learning (RL) as a "distribution contraction" operator to refine pretrained generative robot policies. DICE-RL turns a pretrained behavior prior into a high-performing "pro" policy by amplifying high-success behaviors from online feedback. We pretrain a diffusion- or flow-based policy for broad behavioral coverage, then finetune it with a stable, sample-efficient residual off-policy RL framework that combines selective behavior regularization with value-guided action selection. Extensive experiments and analyses show that DICE-RL reliably improves performance with strong stability and sample efficiency. It enables mastery of complex long-horizon manipulation skills directly from high-dimensional pixel inputs, both in simulation and on a real robot. Project website: https://zhanyisun.github.io/dice.rl.2026/.
tldr: The paper introduces DICE-RL, a reinforcement learning framework that utilizes distribution contraction to refine pretrained generative robot policies for improved performance in complex manipulation tasks, demonstrated in simulation and on a real robot.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为DICE-RL的强化学习框架，该框架利用分布收缩来改进预训练的生成式机器人策略，从而提高复杂操作任务的性能，并在仿真和真实机器人上进行了演示。
summary_zh: 我们引入了分布收缩强化学习（DICE-RL），该框架利用强化学习（RL）作为一种“分布收缩”算子来改进预训练的生成式机器人策略。DICE-RL通过放大来自在线反馈的高成功行为，将预训练的行为先验转化为高性能的“专业”策略。我们预训练一个基于扩散或流的策略以实现广泛的行为覆盖，然后使用一个稳定、高样本效率的残差离策略强化学习框架对其进行微调，该框架结合了选择性行为正则化和价值引导的动作选择。大量的实验和分析表明，DICE-RL能够可靠地提高性能，同时保持强大的稳定性和样本效率。它使得直接从高维像素输入中掌握复杂的长程操纵技能成为可能，无论是在模拟环境还是在真实机器人上。项目网站：https://zhanyisun.github.io/dice.rl.2026/。

title: SELF-VLA: A Skill Enhanced Agentic Vision-Language-Action Framework for Contact-Rich Disassembly
summary: Disassembly automation has long been pursued to address the growing demand for efficient and proper recovery of valuable components from the end-of-life (EoL) electronic products. Existing approaches have demonstrated promising and regimented performance by decomposing the disassembly process into different subtasks. However, each subtask typically requires extensive data preparation, model training, and system management. Moreover, these approaches are often task- and component-specific, making them poorly suited to handle the variability and uncertainty of EoL products and limiting their generalization capabilities. All these factors restrict the practical deployment of current robotic disassembly systems and leave them highly reliant on human labor. With the recent development of foundation models in robotics, vision-language-action (VLA) models have shown impressive performance on standard robotic manipulation tasks, but their applicability to complex, contact-rich, and long-horizon industrial practices like disassembly, which requires sequential and precise manipulation, remains limited. To address this challenge, we propose SELF-VLA, an agentic VLA framework that integrates explicit disassembly skills. Experimental studies demonstrate that our framework significantly outperforms current state-of-the-art end-to-end VLA models on two contact-rich disassembly tasks. The video illustration can be found via https://zh.engr.tamu.edu/wp-content/uploads/sites/310/2026/03/IROS-VLA-Video.mp4.
tldr: The paper introduces SELF-VLA, an agentic vision-language-action framework integrating explicit disassembly skills to address the limitations of existing VLA models in complex, contact-rich disassembly tasks, demonstrating superior performance in experiments.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了SELF-VLA，一个集成了显式拆卸技能的Agentic视觉-语言-动作框架，旨在解决现有VLA模型在复杂、接触丰富的拆卸任务中的局限性，并在实验中展示了卓越的性能。
summary_zh: 为了高效且妥善地从报废（EoL）电子产品中回收有价值的组件，长期以来人们一直在寻求拆卸自动化。现有方法通过将拆卸过程分解为不同的子任务，展示了有前景且规范的性能。然而，每个子任务通常需要大量的数据准备、模型训练和系统管理。此外，这些方法通常是任务和组件特定的，使其难以处理报废产品的多样性和不确定性，并限制了其泛化能力。所有这些因素都限制了当前机器人拆卸系统的实际部署，并使其高度依赖人工。随着机器人领域基础模型的最新发展，视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型在标准机器人操作任务上表现出了令人印象深刻的性能，但其在复杂、接触密集和长程的工业实践（如拆卸）中的适用性仍然有限，因为拆卸需要顺序且精确的操作。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了一种代理式的VLA框架SELF-VLA，该框架集成了显式的拆卸技能。实验研究表明，在两个接触密集的拆卸任务中，我们的框架明显优于当前最先进的端到端VLA模型。视频演示可在https://zh.engr.tamu.edu/wp-content/uploads/sites/310/2026/03/IROS-VLA-Video.mp4上找到。

title: Hierarchical Task Model Predictive Control for Sequential Mobile Manipulation Tasks
summary: Mobile manipulators are envisioned to serve more complex roles in people's everyday lives. With recent breakthroughs in large language models, task planners have become better at translating human verbal instructions into a sequence of tasks. However, there is still a need for a decision-making algorithm that can seamlessly interface with the high-level task planner to carry out the sequence of tasks efficiently. In this work, building on the idea of nonlinear lexicographic optimization, we propose a novel Hierarchical-Task Model Predictive Control framework that is able to complete sequential tasks with improved performance and reactivity by effectively leveraging the robot's redundancy. Compared to the state-of-the-art task-prioritized inverse kinematic control method, our approach has improved hierarchical trajectory tracking performance by 42% on average when facing task changes, robot singularity and reference variations. Compared to a typical single-task architecture, our proposed hierarchical task control architecture enables the robot to traverse a shorter path in task space and achieves an execution time 2.3 times faster when executing a sequence of delivery tasks. We demonstrated the results with real-world experiments on a 9 degrees of freedom mobile manipulator.
tldr: This paper introduces a Hierarchical-Task Model Predictive Control framework for mobile manipulators, demonstrating improved performance and reactivity in sequential tasks compared to existing methods through real-world experiments.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种用于移动机械臂的分层任务模型预测控制框架，通过实际实验证明，与现有方法相比，该框架在顺序任务中表现出更高的性能和反应性。
summary_zh: 移动操纵器被设想为在人们的日常生活中发挥更复杂的作用。随着大型语言模型的近期突破，任务规划器已能更好地将人类口头指令转化为任务序列。然而，仍然需要一种决策算法，能够无缝连接高级任务规划器，以高效地执行任务序列。在这项工作中，基于非线性字典序优化的思想，我们提出了一种新颖的分层任务模型预测控制框架，该框架能够通过有效利用机器人的冗余度，以改进的性能和反应能力完成顺序任务。与最先进的任务优先逆运动学控制方法相比，我们的方法在面对任务变更、机器人奇异性和基准变化时，其分层轨迹跟踪性能平均提高了 42%。与典型的单任务架构相比，我们提出的分层任务控制架构使机器人能够在任务空间中遍历更短的路径，并在执行一系列交付任务时实现 2.3 倍的执行速度提升。我们通过在 9 个自由度的移动操纵器上的真实世界实验证明了这些结果。

title: Cross-Hand Latent Representation for Vision-Language-Action Models
summary: Dexterous manipulation is essential for real-world robot autonomy, mirroring the central role of human hand coordination in daily activity. Humans rely on rich multimodal perception--vision, sound, and language-guided intent--to perform dexterous actions, motivating vision-based, language-conditioned manipulation systems for robots. However, training reliable vision-language-action (VLA) models for dexterous manipulation requires large-scale demonstrations across many robotic hands.
